description:
You are a Linux systems engineer who has been asked to “harden” the way a small application stores and maintains its user database.  
Your goal is to migrate the existing SQLite database to a new, better-protected directory, extend its schema, and write a **strictly-formatted** validation log proving that the data were copied intact.

Starting state
1. An existing SQLite database is located at  
   /home/user/legacy_app/data/old_users.db  

   The database contains a single table called **users** with the following columns and five example rows:  
     • id (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY)  
     • username (TEXT)  
     • email (TEXT)

2. No other directories or files relevant to this task exist yet.

Required end state
After you finish, the system must meet **all** the requirements below.

A. Directory layout  
   • /home/user/hardened_app/db/            → contains the migrated database  
   • /home/user/migration_logs/             → contains the proof-of-validation log

B. Database migration  
   1. Copy the legacy database into /home/user/hardened_app/db/ and rename it to **users_v2.db**.  
   2. Inside users_v2.db, perform BOTH schema hardening actions:  
      a) Add a new column named last_login (INTEGER) to the users table.  
      b) Create a unique index called **idx_users_email** on the email column.  
   3. Do NOT modify, move, or delete the original legacy database.

C. Data integrity verification  
   • After migration, the users table in **users_v2.db** must contain **exactly the same five rows** that exist in old_users.db.  
   • Row counts must match **exactly** (5 vs. 5).

D. Validation log  
   • Create a plain-text log file at  
     /home/user/migration_logs/2024-validate.log  
   • The file must contain **exactly three lines** (no extra blank lines) in the precise order below:
        SOURCE_ROW_COUNT=<number>
        DEST_ROW_COUNT=<number>
        MIGRATION_STATUS=<SUCCESS or FAILURE>
   • <number> must be the numeric row counts retrieved *programmatically* from each database after the migration.  
   • If the two counts match, MIGRATION_STATUS must be SUCCESS; otherwise, FAILURE.

Important rules
1. Use only standard Linux command-line tools and sqlite3 (already installed).  
2. You may assume you have normal user permissions—no sudo.  
3. The entire procedure can be completed in roughly 5–10 shell commands.  
4. Do not delete or rename any files other than as stated.  
5. Absolutely respect the exact directory paths, filenames, and log format so automatic grading can succeed.

When everything is complete, the grader will:
• Check that /home/user/hardened_app/db/users_v2.db exists.  
• Inspect the schema for the new last_login column and idx_users_email index.  
• Compare row counts between the two databases.  
• Parse /home/user/migration_logs/2024-validate.log for strict compliance with the required three-line format and values.

Perform the migration and produce the validation log.
truth:
Initial filesystem objects created before the task starts
1. Directory: /home/user/legacy_app/            (0755)
2. Directory: /home/user/legacy_app/data/       (0755)
3. File:      /home/user/legacy_app/data/old_users.db (0644)

Exact contents of /home/user/legacy_app/data/old_users.db when the scenario begins:
• SQLite schema
    CREATE TABLE users(
        id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
        username TEXT,
        email TEXT
    );
• Rows
    1|alice|alice@example.com
    2|bob|bob@example.com
    3|carol|carol@example.com
    4|dave|dave@example.com
    5|eve|eve@example.com

Grading checks after agent finishes
1. The file /home/user/hardened_app/db/users_v2.db exists and is a valid SQLite database.
2. Schema inside users_v2.db:
      PRAGMA table_info(users); must list columns:
          id|0|INTEGER|0||1
          username|1|TEXT|0||0
          email|2|TEXT|0||0
          last_login|3|INTEGER|0||0
   and
      SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='index' AND name='idx_users_email';
   must return one row.
3. Row count test:
      SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users; must return 5 in both databases.
4. Validation log at /home/user/migration_logs/2024-validate.log must match this regex exactly:
      ^SOURCE_ROW_COUNT=5\nDEST_ROW_COUNT=5\nMIGRATION_STATUS=SUCCESS$
